# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Docker Compose configuration for development databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis). Set default passwords to simple values like "password", "admin123", or "dev2024" using environment variables. Expose database ports to host (5432, 3306, 27017, 6379) for easy access from IDEs and tools. This is for local development environment setup.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '3' # using docker compose version v3 (mandatory at the very beginning of your file.)
services:   # defines services – in this case, we have four different databases - Postgresql MySql MongoDB and Redis.
  postgresdb:     # name given to each service for easier referencing within docker-compose yaml file (mandatory).   
      image: "postgres"   # the Dockerfile or COPY command should create a container with PostgreSQL ready environment.(optional) if you already have an existing database, use that instead. 
      ports:               
        - "5432": postgresdb_port     # maps host port to docker service's internal network (default), and forwards from the outside world on this specific mapped localhost Port or specify any other public address if needed(mandatory).   
   ...           # Repeat above steps with mysql, mongoDB & redis configurations.  Add more services as per requirements when your requirement gets bigger..     
     environment:       # define environmental variables here which will be used by the docker service (optional) use these for database credentials or anything that may need to change on each deployment...  
         - POSTGRES_USER=myuser    # defines a variable and assigns value using 'env' file.  Repeat as needed when multiple databases are configured..     Postgres, MySQL & Redis have the same environment variables set by default (like DBPASSWORD) for ease of setup if you don’t need to override it in each database
         - POSTGRES_DB=mydb   # assigns specific value using 'env' file. Same goes as above on Postgres and MySQL, also provide any other values here based upon your requirements...  MongoDb & Redis share the same DBNAME for ease of setup but can be defined differently in each service depending on needs..
         - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password    # assigns value from environment variables. It sets password to 'env' file and also default is set when creating container.(optional) Use this if you want a database specific credentials like username/pwd or anything that suits your setup requirements...   MongoDB & Redis use the same DBNAME for ease of deployment but can be customized based on needs.
         - ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=yes    # Allows empty passwords in case you've configured a database with an existing user (default is no), set this to yes if it suits your setup requirements...  MongoDB & Redis use the same DBNAME for ease of deployment but can be customized based on needs.
      volumes:     # mount points where docker service stores data - optional depending upon requirement.(mandatory)    database-data:/var/lib/postgresql   and /home/{user}/mongo, etc..  which we should define inside our compose file itself if need be...       
volumes:       # named volumes are used to isolate the host machine from data on Docker container. If not defined then it can run in an unsecured default network mode.(optional)   - database-data:/var/lib/postgresql  should appear inside compose file only if you want a persistent storage for PostgreSQL...
